General Election 2054 — Caprica

The republic
belongs to all of us.

Res Publica is a civic republican party built on accountability, transparency, and the belief that public institutions exist to serve the public — not the other way around.

Our platform

What we stand for

Accountability

Public institutions must answer to the people they serve. We will establish independent oversight with real enforcement power — not frameworks designed to absorb complaints without acting on them.

Environmental Justice

The Verdoyante disaster was not an accident of fate. It was the result of ignored warnings and inadequate regulation. We will rebuild the inspection frameworks that failed Pleucadeuc.

Real Security

Security is not a slogan. The ManMan Centre attack exposed a chain of command that collapsed under pressure. Res Publica will demand a full independent inquiry and structural reform.

Workers' Dignity

A worker who files a safety complaint should never wonder whether it will disappear. Mandatory response timelines, enforceable standards, and the right to organise without fear.

Regional Voice

Republicanism without regional representation is just centralism with better rhetoric. Every constituency deserves a representative who is present, not one who arrives only at election time.

Civic Foundation

The republic is not a word. It is a promise — that the institutions of this country were built for the people inside it. We intend to keep that promise.

Two crises. One government. No answers.

The Gallen spill. The ManMan Centre attack. In both cases, warnings were ignored, systems failed, and people paid the price. Avenir Caprica is asking you to reward that record. We are asking you to change it.

54,000
cubic metres of cyanide water released into the Verdoyante
24
lives lost in the ManMan Centre attack
140M
argents of economic damage over the coming decade
2 yrs
of ignored worker complaints before the dam burst
Res Publica
For the Republic. For the People.
General Election 2054 · Caprica
Authorised by Res Publica Central Council
Our people

The candidates of
Res Publica

Men and women from across Caprica, standing for their communities in the 2054 general election.

Presidential Candidate
DA
Draqi Arkas
Presidential Candidate
Republic of Caprica — National
Res Publica's candidate for the presidency of Caprica. Running on a platform of accountability, institutional reform, and a renewed civic republican compact for all Caprican citizens.
Party Leadership
KP
Kostas Pastry
Founder & Party Leader
Ventura Constituency
Founder of Res Publica and candidate for the Ventura constituency seat. A believer that leadership must be local before it can be national — present on Roundhead Street, and committed to being present in Parliament.
Constituency Candidates
KP
Kostas Pastry
MP Candidate
Ventura
Running for the Ventura constituency seat. Born and based in Ventura, Kostas has spent the campaign on the ground — serving at the Central Kitchen on Roundhead Street and speaking directly to residents about what this city deserves from its representative.
EV
Eduard Valcourt
MP Candidate
Pleucadeuc — Avenir Caprica Stronghold
Born and raised in Pleucadeuc, Eduard is running in Avenir Caprica's own stronghold — the constituency most devastated by the Gallen spill. A local voice demanding that the people who lost their livelihoods on the Verdoyante are not forgotten when the cameras move on.
KA
Klinton Arkas
MP Candidate
Carnapol
Son of presidential candidate Draqi Arkas. A union man turned political economist, Klinton brings both the language of the shop floor and the analytical tools of academic study to Carnapol's working-class constituency. Running to be the worker's voice in Parliament.
PO
Paul Otrione
MP Candidate
Pittston
Coming from a union hall background, Paul is a staunch advocate for ensuring workers receive what their labor is worth. He aims to fix the structural gaps in public institutions where safety complaints disappear into bureaucracies designed to absorb rather than respond.
Res Publica
For the Republic. For the People.
